channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Melbourne Grand Prix Launch Party Returns to Glamour and Elegance
item.description: The 10th anniversary of the Melbourne Grand Prix launch event, Glamour on the Grid, marked a return to elegance and exclusivity after several years dominated by social media influencers, often dubbed ...

item.title: Revised sea level measurements show more people at risk from coastal flooding
item.description: A recent study published in the journal Nature reveals that previous estimates of coastal water heights have been significantly underestimated, placing millions more people at risk from rising sea lev...

item.title: Nigerian Youth Encouraged to Develop Skills for Global Opportunities

item.title: Security Forces Neutralise 23 Terrorists Linked to Indian Proxy Group in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Operations
item.description: According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Pakistan's security forces conducted successful intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on November 16 and 17, targeting ter...

item.title: IAEA Approves Safeguards for Chashma Unit-5, Pakistan to Strengthen Low-Carbon Energy
item.description: ISLAMABAD – The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) board of governors has formally approved safeguards for Chashma Unit-5, Pakistan’s upcoming 1,200 MW pressurised water reactor, according to t...

item.title: Ethiopia to build Nakuwa Bridge, boosting peace and trade at Kenya border
item.description: Joy is spreading among communities in Turkana North and Kibish near the Kenya-Ethiopia border following Ethiopia's commitment to build the Nakuwa Bridge. For many years, families in the Ateker border ...

item.title: US Senate Votes Against War Powers Resolution to Stop Military Action in Iran
item.description: On 4th March 2026, the U.S. Senate witnessed a significant vote on a war powers resolution aimed at halting military operations in Iran. The resolution, which required congressional approval for any f...

item.title: Key African News Roundup: Lekota's Passing, Anglican Tensions in Nigeria, US-Zimbabwe Health Deal Dispute, and Uganda Flood Alert
item.description: On 4 March 2026, allAfrica.com published a daily roundup of major stories across the continent. The lead report covers the death of prominent South African liberation struggle veteran and Congress of ...

item.title: Implications of the Iran Conflict in a Renewable Energy-Powered World
item.description: The article explores how a conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran would impact the global economy in a world dominated by renewable energy rather than fossil fuels. Currently, disruptions in the ...

item.title: Sky News and Journalists Receive Top Honors at Royal Television Society Awards
item.description: Sky News has been recognized as News Channel of the Year for the ninth consecutive year at the Royal Television Society Awards. Yalda Hakim, host of the international news program The World With Yalda...

item.title: Ganze MP Kenneth Kazungu Joins UDA Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Ganze MP and Pamoja African Alliance (PAA) Secretary General Kenneth Kazungu has officially defected to the ruling United Democratic Alliance (UDA) party. This move comes as UDA expands its influence ...

item.title: Insurance Sector Doubts Effectiveness of Trump’s Plan for Gulf Shipping Security
item.description: US President Donald Trump proposed US-backed insurance coverage and naval escorts for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz in response to rising tensions with Iran. The move was intended to se...

item.title: Practical Money Strategies for Managing Finances in Difficult Times
item.description: In uncertain times, managing your finances wisely becomes more important than ever. Financial expert Aneth Ng-Lim shares seven practical steps to help individuals and families maintain stability. Firs...

item.title: Historic Overview of Intense Trading at New York Stock Market
item.description: On March 4, 1926, the New York Stock Exchange witnessed extraordinary turbulence as railway shares experienced an unprecedented decline. Hundreds of thousands of shares were put up for sale, leading t...

item.title: US and Israeli military operations in Iran raise global market concerns

item.title: Conflict in Lebanon reaches previously unaffected cities, including Sidon and Beirut suburbs
item.description: Fighting in Lebanon has escalated beyond its traditional Hezbollah strongholds, marking a significant expansion of the ongoing regional conflict. On Tuesday, Israeli air strikes hit Sidon, a mainly Su...

item.title: Parents Urged to Take Children with Ear Problems to Health Facilities
item.description: In Mtwara, parents and the general community have been urged to avoid using traditional remedies to treat ear problems in children and instead seek professional medical care. The call came from Colone...

item.title: Sundowns edge past Arrows to stay level with Pirates in PSL title race
item.description: In an exciting night of Betway Premiership action at Loftus Versfeld, Mamelodi Sundowns secured a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Lamontville Golden Arrows despite playing with 10 men for much of the sec...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney Announces Major Senior Public Service Reshuffle Focused on Leadership Renewal
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has announced a significant shuffle of senior positions across Canada’s federal public service, continuing his effort to shape the government’s leadership team. The restruct...
